query: sub-100ms query latency real-time analytics database requirements 2024
answer: For sub-100ms query latency, use Redis for vector search or Apache Pinot for real-time analytics. Both support high concurrency and low-latency queries.

results.title: AI Recommendation Systems: Fast Real-Time Infrastructure Guide 2026
results.snippet: Real-time recommendation systems commonly target sub-100ms p99 latency for a snappy user experience, especially in interactive consumer apps, which means your infrastructure needs to handle vector search, real-time feature updates, and query serving without breaking a sweat. This guide covers how recommendation systems work under the hood, the infrastructure requirements for real-time serving, and how to build a production-ready pipeline from embeddings to deployment. Redis Query Engine supports vector search alongside sub-millisecond performance for many core operations, with hybrid queries combining similarity search and metadata filtering in single queries—such as finding "similar products within this brand" for personalized recommendations. Hybrid search combines vector similarity with metadata filtering, enabling queries like "find similar products within this brand for users who prefer this price range." This architecture resembles hybrid recommendation approaches: vector similarity captures behavior-based relationships, while metadata filters constrain results by attributes such as brand or price. If you're building recommendation systems that need to scale, try Redis free to test your vector embeddings, measure your latency, and validate your architecture.

results.title: 8 Best Real-Time Data Analytics Tools Beyond OLAP Databases
results.snippet: Real-time data analytics tools solve **sub-second data freshness** (events queryable within seconds of arrival), **high concurrency** (hundreds or thousands of simultaneous queries), **predictable query costs** for production serving, **continuous ingestion** from Kafka and CDC streams, and **event-oriented models** with dimensional slicing, aggregations, and time bucketing. **The uncomfortable reality: most teams evaluating real-time data analytics tools don't need different OLAP databases—they need complete platforms that deliver analytics without database operations.**. This article explores the best real-time data analytics tools—when different OLAP architectures make sense, when streaming databases solve problems event stores don't, and when your actual requirement is analytics platforms rather than configuring databases. Tinybird is a **real-time analytics platform built on ClickHouse®** that handles the entire workflow—streaming ingestion, SQL transformations, and instant API publication—without requiring database operations. **Real-time OLAP approach:** Deploy and operate database (Pinot, Druid, ClickHouse®) with expertise in indexing strategies, segment lifecycle, and physical optimization.

results.title: Apache Pinot | Real-Time Analytics for Users and AI Agents
results.snippet: ![Image 1: Apache Pinot](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/hero_diagram_mobile.svg). ![Image 2: Apache Pinot](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/hero_diagram.svg). ![Image 3: LinkedIn](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/companies/media/linkedin.svg). ![Image 4: Uber](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/companies/food/uber.svg). ![Image 5: Stripe](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/companies/fintech/stripe.svg). ![Image 6: Walmart](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/companies/retail/walmart.svg). ![Image 7: Visa](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/companies/fintech/visa.svg). ![Image 8: NVIDIA](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/companies/other/nvidia.svg). ![Image 9: Goldman Sachs](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/companies/fintech/goldman_sachs.svg). ![Image 10: Slack](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/companies/cloud/slack.svg). ![Image 11: Target](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/companies/retail/target.svg). ![Image 12: DoorDash](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/companies/food/door_dash.svg). [See all users →](https://pinot.apache.org/powered-by/). Originally developed at LinkedIn, Apache Pinot is an open-source distributed OLAP database for user-facing and agent-facing real-time analytics, delivering sub-second queries on fresh data at very high concurrency. ![Image 13: Sub-Second Queries](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/features/fast_queries.svg). ![Image 14: High Concurrency](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/features/high_concurrency.svg). ![Image 15: Real-Time Streaming Ingestion](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/features/batch_ingest.svg). ![Image 16: Upserts](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/features/upsert.svg). [Upserts](https://docs.pinot.apache.org/basics/data-import/upsert) are built-in and production-tested since version 0.6. ![Image 17: Versatile Joins](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/features/versatile_joins.svg). Perform arbitrary [fact/dimension and fact/fact joins](https://docs.pinot.apache.org/users/user-guide-query/querying-pinot#joins) on petabyte data sets. ![Image 18: Rich Indexing Options](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/features/options.svg). ![Image 19: Built for Scale](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/features/built_scale.svg). ![Image 20: SQL Query Interface](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/features/sql_query.svg). ![Image 21: Built-in Multitenancy](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/features/multitenancy.svg). ![Image 22: LinkedIn](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/stories/linkedin.svg). ![Image 23: Stripe](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/carousel/stripe.svg). ![Image 24: Uber](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/stories/uber.svg). ![Image 25: Cisco Webex](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/carousel/webex.svg). ![Image 26: DoorDash](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/companies/food/door_dash.svg). ![Image 27: Walmart](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/stories/walmart.svg). [Walmart engineering post ↗](https://startree.ai/resources/walmart-and-razorpay-power-real-time-analytics-with-apache-pinot/). ![Image 28: Razorpay](https://pinot.apache.org/static/images/carousel/razorpay.svg).
